description: Introduction of Phase 2 to the trine-eval Self-Upgrade spec. Phase 1 (Sprints 1-6) closes 15 gaps against Anthropic's eval-driven-development playbook within existing project-type rubrics. Phase 2 expands the harness's mission to support a new project-type domain ('harness-build' / agent runtime harnesses), sourced from an externally-supplied playbook brought in by the user. This is a Method-axis scope expansion: the product vision in spec.md is being widened to add a new project-type vertical. Phase 2 also entails downstream Material-axis records (new rubric file, traps catalog) which will be classified as scheduled and suppressed when they land within Phase 2 sprint scope.

evidence.claim: Sprint 6 is the most recently active sprint and its declared scope was JIT context retrieval patterns only — Phase 2 / harness-build / agent-runtime rubric are not in its features array.

Pre-Phase-2 spec.md product vision was bounded to 'close 15 identified gaps against the playbook' (Anthropic eval-driven-dev playbook). It did not reference agent-runtime harnesses, harness-build, or any new project-type vertical.
evidence.verification: git show HEAD~1:.harness/spec.md | grep -i 'agent-runtime\|harness-build' || echo 'absent in prior revision'

evidence.claim: The eval-rubric registry currently lists five rubrics and does not include an agent-runtime-harness / harness-build rubric.
The change is user-initiated and deliberate: user explicitly supplied an external playbook document and asked to plan integration sprints. This satisfies the active (henkoten) criterion.
Phase 1 telemetry shows uniform PASS with no failure pattern that would force a domain pivot — corroborating that Phase 2 is additive expansion, not corrective response.

Suppression rule does not apply: Sprint 6 scope (JIT context retrieval) does not include spec.md expansion, rubric-registry expansion, or harness-build project-type work. Phase 2 introduction is therefore unscheduled relative to the active sprint, even though the user intentionally initiated it. Active+unscheduled is a valid combination.

andon_signal.reason: Pre-amendment: spec.md vision and feature list did not authorize Phase 2 work, risking orphaned sprints. Resolved by spec.md amendment + features.json bootstrap before Sprint 7 begins.
evidence: .harness/features.json (was absent — created as part of Phase 2 prep)

resolution_summary: Spec amended with Phase 1 / Phase 2 sections. features.json bootstrapped with 19 entries (16 implemented + 3 planned). Sprints 7-11 appended to sprints.json with council-recommended fixes folded in (unconditional Sprint 7 threshold; Should-NOT gates for prior-deliverable protection; ephemeral Sprint 10; templates/by-rubric/ layout for Sprint 8; playbook_stage field scoped to harness-build mode in Sprint 9).

description: Behavioral-coverage rule was introduced into the contract/grading methodology outside any sprint's declared scope. The 2-way grader split (deterministic/llm-judge) has been replaced with a 3-way split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge), and a new constraint requires behavioral criteria to hold >=60% of total weight unless the contract's Technical Notes justify the exception. Affected files have been edited but not committed at the time of detection. Sprint 7 (harness-build rubric) will inherit this rule as soon as the Generator reads the modified agents/skills. The rule is coherent and intentional per the user's auto-memory dated 2026-05-16, but the rollout bypassed sprint negotiation and council review.

evidence.claim: Seven plugin agent/skill files have uncommitted edits at the moment Sprint 7 begins.
The diffs introduce a 3-way grader split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge) and a >=60% behavioral-coverage requirement, replacing the prior 2-way (deterministic/llm-judge) scheme.
Sprint 6 closed with status PASS and did not include behavioral-coverage rule work in its features array; the rule predates this autorun and falls outside any negotiated sprint contract.
The change is consonant with the harness's mission (eval-driven development) and aligns with Sprint 7's emphasis on behavioral verification for harness-build rubrics; risk of distortion is low.

The user's auto-memory notes the rule was decided on 2026-05-16 ('trine-eval contracts now require >=60% behavioral-criterion weight; 3-way grader split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge) replaces old 2-way'), corroborating that the change is intentional rather than accidental drift.

resolution_summary: Ratified via DEC-0007 (2026-06-01T14:24:00Z) — single-prompt L4 path under nemawashi_walkthrough_version: 0. Sprint 9 schema port (commit 98cfa49) brought the tasks.json schema into conformance with the 3-way grader split, completing the rule downstream into the in-repo audit chain.

description: Sprints 7, 8, and 9 ran the full /trine-eval:harness-sprint loop end-to-end without invoking the council fan-out documented in `.council/config.json` as `review_frequency: every-sprint`. Sprint 7 has a sprint-prebrief file; Sprint 8 and Sprint 9 do not. No post-sprint course-corrections file exists in `.council/course-corrections/` for any of Sprints 7-9. The Phase 1.5 plan insertion (DEC pending) was authored by the orchestrator in a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper + nemawashi. This is a Method-axis drift: the stated cadence is per-sprint, observed cadence is per-three-sprints (and only when the user manually invokes /henkaten-council:council-autorun). The risk is corrective lag: scope-guardian and architect findings about sprints 7-9 have not been recorded, and any drift that occurred has accumulated unaudited across three sprints.

evidence.claim: review_frequency in .council/config.json is set to every-sprint.
Only sprint-07.json exists in .council/sprint-prebrief/; sprint-08.json and sprint-09.json are absent.
decision-log.jsonl contains 7 DEC entries, all from sprint-context 0, 6, or 7; no entries for sprint-context 8 or sprint-context 9.
Sprints 7, 8, 9 all completed PASS at 100% weighted, so the drift was not surfaced by a sprint failure that would otherwise force council attention.

The Phase 1.5 plan amendment (Sprint 9 insertion, sprints 9-11 renumbered to 10-12) was decided unilaterally by the orchestrator within a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper. Reversibility is reversible (git revert of 497f184), but the change is structurally similar to DEC-0002 (spec amendment) and DEC-0004 (sprint reordering) which both went through nemawashi-equivalent ratification.

description: The in-repo plugin source `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-workflow/SKILL.md` documents only Steps 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 — it does not contain Step 0.5 (regression gate), Step 1d (tasks.json emission), or Step 3d (Batch API submission grouping). The Sprint 9 schema port added prose in `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-contract/SKILL.md` at lines 74, 140, 142 referencing these three steps as if they exist in the in-repo workflow. The references resolve only against the cached/published v0.3.3 plugin from which actual harness-sprint execution loads — the in-repo source has silently diverged from the published artifact. Architect and scope-guardian independently confirmed the gap; orchestrator spot-checked via direct grep (logged to audit-log). Andon issued `alert` (not `stop`) because runtime execution against the cached SKILL.md is unaffected; the divergence is documentation correctness, not execution risk. This Henkaten captures the structural discovery: the repo-source workflow has been drifting from the published workflow without registration, and Sprint 9's port now creates dangling references that make the drift visible.

evidence.claim: In-repo sprint-workflow/SKILL.md defines exactly six steps (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) — no Step 0.5, no Step 1d, no Step 3d.
The Sprint 9 schema port wrote three references to non-existent in-repo workflow steps into sprint-contract/SKILL.md at lines 74, 140, 142.
The reference points to a published v0.3.3 cached harness-sprint/SKILL.md that the harness execution path uses — the in-repo source has not been ported to parity.
Sprint 9's declared Out-of-Scope forbids touching any SKILL.md other than sprint-contract/SKILL.md, so the gap cannot be closed inside Sprint 9 — it requires a follow-up scheduled sprint.

Architect (this swarm) issued andon `alert` (not `stop`) because the actual workflow execution loads from cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md which has the steps. Scope-guardian and henkaten-detector concurred. Orchestrator spot-checked the underlying grep claim and logged PASS to audit-log.

Classified as change_origin=passive because the gap has been present continuously since the v0.3.3 / repo-source divergence began; Sprint 9 did not introduce the gap, only surfaced it through new prose that references the missing steps.

andon_signal.reason: Documentation references in-repo workflow steps that do not exist in the in-repo workflow file; runtime execution against the cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md is unaffected, so this is documentation correctness, not execution risk. Resolved within takt budget — all three council agents (architect, scope-guardian, henkaten-detector) concurred alert is the correct level.
